# stalwart_group (Resource)

Manages a Stalwart group (the `Account` JMAP object with `@type` `Group`). Group membership is managed from the member side, via the `member_of` attribute on `stalwart_account`, mirroring the underlying API.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "stalwart_group" "team" {
  domain_id   = stalwart_domain.example.id
  name        = "team"
  description = "All staff"
}

# Membership is managed from the account side via member_of:
resource "stalwart_account" "alice" {
  domain_id = stalwart_domain.example.id
  name      = "alice"
  member_of = [stalwart_group.team.id]
}

# Import an existing group by its email address:
# terraform import stalwart_group.team team@example.com
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Group name — the local part of the email address. Changing it replaces the group.

### Optional

- `description` (String) Human-readable description of the group.
- `domain` (String) Name of the domain this object belongs to, e.g. `example.com`. Resolved to a domain id at apply time. Mutually exclusive with `domain_id`.
- `domain_id` (String) Opaque id of the domain this object belongs to. Mutually exclusive with `domain`.
- `quota` (Number) Maximum disk space allocated to the group, in bytes (maps to `maxDiskQuota`).
- `role` (String) Built-in role for the group: `Default` or `Custom`. Defaults to `Default`.
- `role_ids` (Set of String) Custom role ids assigned to the group, used when `role` is `Custom`.

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (String) Creation timestamp of the group.
- `email_address` (String) Full email address of the group, formed as `name@domain` (server-set).
- `id` (String) Opaque server-assigned identifier of the group.
- `used_disk_quota` (Number) Disk space currently used by the group, in bytes (server-set).

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Import by email address (preferred):
terraform import stalwart_group.team team@example.com

# Import by opaque server-assigned id:
# terraform import stalwart_group.team <opaque-id>
```
